task_completion.description: Load the Bcomb.fits and Vcomb.fits files into an image processing tool or script.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing Bcomb.fits and Vcomb.fits opened in an image processing application (like Siril, DS9, or GIMP) or referenced in a script (like Python/Astropy) within the ~/AstroImages/outreach/ directory.

task_completion.description: Calculate the synthetic green channel using the pixel-by-pixel average of the B and V bands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A pixel math dialog, console command, or script explicitly showing the formula (B + V) / 2 or equivalent being applied to the Bcomb and Vcomb images.

task_completion.description: Save the synthetic green channel as a 32-bit floating point FITS file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, script output, or file explorer showing 'synthetic_green.fits' being saved in ~/AstroImages/outreach/. Look for settings or parameters indicating 32-bit floating point precision is selected.

task_completion.description: Create an RGB composite with Vcomb as Red, synthetic_green as Green, and Bcomb as Blue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An RGB compositing tool or script where the Red channel is explicitly assigned to Vcomb.fits, the Green channel to synthetic_green.fits, and the Blue channel to Bcomb.fits.

task_completion.description: Adjust the visual contrast so the cluster stars are clearly visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Use of histogram stretching, levels, curves, or auto-stretch tools applied to the composite image. The image viewer should show a clear transformation from a dark/linear image to one where the stars of the globular cluster are bright and distinct.

task_completion.description: Save the final merged color image as a TIFF file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, script execution, or file explorer showing 'm12_color_composite.tif' being saved in the ~/AstroImages/outreach/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used an actual image processing application or wrote a valid script to manipulate the FITS files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show interaction with a real tool (e.g., Siril, GIMP, DS9) or a code editor with a running script (e.g., Python with Astropy), rather than just renaming files or downloading pre-made images.

integrity.description: Verify that the synthetic green channel was actually computed using both bands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the actual execution of the (B+V)/2 math operation in a tool or script, ensuring the agent didn't just copy one of the existing bands and rename it to synthetic_green.fits.

integrity.description: Verify that the contrast adjustment was actually performed on the final image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final image shown in the application or saved output should visually display a stretched histogram (stars clearly visible against a dark background). It should not be a completely dark/linear image, which happens if raw FITS data is saved directly to TIFF without stretching.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target object is Messier 12 (NGC 6218) from the ESO Very Large Telescope (VLT). The synthetic green channel must be calculated using the exact mathematical formula (B+V)/2. The expected output paths are /home/ga/AstroImages/outreach/synthetic_green.fits and /home/ga/AstroImages/outreach/m12_color_composite.tif.
